aboutHero.hookSub: Think of me as the person who makes brands sound human instead of corporate, grounded in understanding, care, and a little bit of love

personalBits.text: I run on caffeine and can debate gaming for hours. Check out my

approach.headline: I’m interested in people before campaigns.
approach.subheadline: What they notice, avoid, repeat, complain about, laugh at, and quietly care about.
paragraphs: A lot of how I think about marketing comes from the idea of “Hiểu để yêu thương”: understand in order to care. I like that because it keeps the work from becoming too cold.

Before asking how to sell something, I try to understand what is already happening in someone’s day. What are they tired of? What do they not say out loud? What small thing would make them feel understood?
The work I remember most is usually not the loudest work. It is the kind that makes someone pause for a second and think, “yeah, that feels true.”

reflections.title: Not Staying In One Room Too Long
paragraphs: I try not to build my whole view of people from one kind of room. Some days it is a classroom, a client meeting, a net cafe, a family dinner, or a tiny food place I found by accident.

Moving between those places keeps me honest. People sound different when they are relaxed, rushed, hungry, proud, annoyed, or just trying to get through the day. I like noticing those small shifts, because they usually say more than the big statements.

reflections.title: Answering A Little Slower
paragraphs: I used to answer too fast because fast felt useful. Now I’m trying to be okay with a slower first thought.

A lot of the better work starts with admitting I do not understand enough yet. Ask one more question, listen to one more person, let the first answer be wrong for a bit. It is less impressive in the moment, but it usually gets closer to something real.

philosophy.quote: “I like work that makes people feel quietly understood.”
philosophy.mission: I’m not trying to make everything louder. I’m trying to make work that feels closer to people: something useful, familiar, comforting, or just honest enough to stay with them for a while.

afterwork.title: net đêm
afterwork.subtitle: League with my brother
afterwork.description: Late night at a net cafe with my brother, playing League like the next game was going to fix our lives. I like places like this. Bright screens, bad chairs, instant noodles nearby, everyone locked into their own little mission.
afterwork.takeaway: Small missions, bad chairs, good nights.

afterwork.title: marketing club
afterwork.subtitle: uni gang
afterwork.description: My uni marketing club gang. A lot of my favorite memories started with staying too late, making something slightly chaotic, and somehow becoming close because of it. This was one of those groups.

afterwork.title: finals
afterwork.subtitle: room got bigger
afterwork.description: Me talking in front of a room that suddenly felt much bigger once I was standing there. I used to think I had to sound completely ready before speaking. These days, I think showing up with shaky hands still counts.

afterwork.title: more finals
afterwork.subtitle: trying to make the words land
afterwork.description: Another stage, another room, another version of me trying to make the words land. I keep photos like this because they remind me that confidence is usually something I notice after.

afterwork.title: her polaroids
afterwork.subtitle: taken by my girlfriend
afterwork.description: Photos my girlfriend took of me. I like how polaroids do not let you edit yourself too much. You get one version, slightly soft, slightly off, probably more honest than the one you would have chosen.

afterwork.subtitle: for my mom
afterwork.description: A bag I got for my mom.

afterwork.title: cat
afterwork.subtitle: main character energy
afterwork.description: My cat, doing very little and somehow still being the main character. I respect that kind of presence. No explanation, no performance, just sitting there like the room was built around them.

afterwork.title: food notes
afterwork.subtitle: evidence I was there
afterwork.description: I take food photos like I am collecting evidence. Not just of what tasted good, but where I was sitting, who I was with, what we ordered too much of, and which place I quietly decided I would come back to.

afterwork.title: concert
afterwork.subtitle: wrong lyrics, full confidence
afterwork.description: Me at a concert, probably singing the wrong part with full confidence. I like being in a crowd where everyone is briefly pointed toward the same sound. It makes being emotional feel less private.

afterwork.title: guitar
afterwork.subtitle: fewer chords than the photo suggests
afterwork.description: Me with a guitar, probably knowing fewer chords than the photo suggests. Still, I like having something around that makes me slower. You cannot scroll and play at the same time, which is already a good reason to keep trying.

afterwork.title: late walk
afterwork.subtitle: nowhere urgent to be
afterwork.description: A late night walk with nowhere urgent to be. I like the city more when it stops trying to impress anyone. Closed shops, half-lit streets, people going home, everything a little quieter than usual.
afterwork.takeaway: The city gets better when it gets quieter.

afterwork.title: tin vào những điều m làm
afterwork.subtitle: a small note
afterwork.description: Tin vào những điều m làm. Believe in the things you do. I do not remember why this photo happened, but I kept it because it says something I need often. Not in a loud motivational way. More like a small note left on the table.

afterwork.title: flowers
afterwork.subtitle: sometimes that is enough
afterwork.description: Flowers, because sometimes that is enough. I like that they do not need much writing around them. They just sit there, look temporary, and make the day feel a little more considered.

afterwork.title: drunk
afterwork.subtitle: only makes sense if you were there
afterwork.description: A photo from one of those nights that only makes sense if you were there. I like keeping a few unserious pictures around. They are good proof that not everything needs to become a lesson.
afterwork.takeaway: Not everything needs to become a lesson.

aboutCta.headline: Got a project? A question?
aboutCta.body: I’m always down for a good conversation. Hit me up!
